topic: Claude Sonnet 5 Review: 82.1% SWE-bench, Dev Team Mode & Pricing Guide

key_points: Claude Sonnet 5 (Fennec) released February 3, 2026
82.1% SWE-bench Verified — first model to break 80% barrier
Priced at $3/1M input, $15/1M output — same as Sonnet 4.5
1M token context window
Agentic workflow optimization with Dev Team Mode
Sonnet 5 outperforms Opus 4.5 at significantly lower cost
Introduces Anthropic Agent Teams — parallel sub-agent orchestration
Optimized for Claude Code and autonomous development workflows
80.2% SWE-bench at 40% lower cost than Opus 4.6
Verified by independent testing firm Vals AI
Developer-focused breakdown of benchmark results
Real-world code editing error rate dropped to 0% on internal benchmarks
Dramatically faster inference than Opus 4.5
Designed for production coding and extended autonomous work
Context retention over 30-hour multi-step coding sessions
Codename Fennec leaked before official release
Built on Google TPUv6 architecture via Antigravity compute layer
Supports spawning specialized sub-agents (Backend, QA, Technical Writer)
Parallel execution reduces overall task completion time
1M token context enables full repository-level understanding
What leaked as Sonnet 5 was officially released as Claude Sonnet 4.6
Sonnet 5 naming retained in community due to benchmark leap
Cost-per-performance ratio makes it best value in mid-tier model market

Batch API halves both input and output costs

stats.fact: Claude Sonnet 5 achieved 82.1% on SWE-bench Verified, the first model to surpass the 80% threshold
Pricing: $3 per 1M input tokens, $15 per 1M output tokens — same price as Claude Sonnet 4.5 but significantly higher performance
1M token context window (approximately 750,000 words) available at standard pricing
Prompt caching for Sonnet 5/4.6: cached reads cost $0.30/MTok vs $3.00/MTok standard — up to 90% input cost reduction
Batch API saves 50% on both input and output token costs
Enterprise customers report 44% reduction in vulnerability intake time and 25% accuracy boost in cybersecurity use cases
stats.source: AWS Blog: Introducing Claude Sonnet 4.5 in Amazon Bedrock

stats.fact: Claude Sonnet 5 outperforms Gemini 3 on SWE-bench: 82.1% vs 63.8% — an 18-point gap
In independent blind human evaluations Q1 2026, Claude-generated content preferred 47% of the time vs 29% GPT-5 and 24% Gemini
Code editing error rate: reduced from 9% (Sonnet 4) to 0% on internal Anthropic benchmarks
stats.source: Anthropic internal benchmarks reported via DevOps.com 2026

stats.fact: Claude Sonnet 5 demonstrated 30+ hour sustained focus on multi-step autonomous coding tasks
stats.source: InfoQ: Claude Sonnet 4.5 Tops SWE-Bench Verified
